summaryZh: 车厢内保持安静是基本共识。通话、大声说话、吃东西都会被侧目——这不是规定，但是共同默契。
summaryEn: Quiet is the default. Calls, loud talking, and eating will get disapproving looks — it's social convention, not law.
pointsZh: 车内手机调静音，不要通话
优先席（シルバーシート）附近建议关闭手机信号
背包高峰期请放到身前或架上
pointsEn: Phones on silent, no voice calls
Near priority seats, turn signal off during rush hour
Rush hour: move backpack to front or overhead rack
tipZh: Suica / PASMO 充值后几乎通行全国地铁与便利店。
tipEn: A Suica or PASMO IC card works nationwide for transit and convenience stores.

titleZh: エレベーター・电梯礼仪
summaryZh: 靠近按钮的人默认是「操作员」：代大家按楼层、按住「开门」键让别人先出，自己最后出。
summaryEn: Whoever stands closest to the panel is the de-facto operator — they press floors, hold the door, and exit last.
pointsZh: 到楼层时按住「開」键（▷|◁ 标志），让其他人先出，自己最后出
进电梯时也按「開」让外面的人先进
pointsEn: On arrival, hold OPEN (▷|◁) and let everyone exit first; you exit last
Hold OPEN while others board when boarding too
tipZh: 「開」是开门键，「閉」是关门键——别按错。对着正在进出的人按「閉」是相当失礼的行为。
tipEn: 「開」opens, 「閉」closes — don't mix them up. Pressing 「閉」on someone entering or leaving is considered very rude.

summaryZh: 边走边吃在日本普遍被视为失礼。尤其在车站、便利店门口和住宅街会很显眼。
summaryEn: Walking while eating is widely seen as impolite. It stands out near stations, convenience stores, and residential streets.
pointsZh: 便利店外通常有立食台或长椅，买完站着或坐着吃完再走
电车站内・月台・车厢里都不要吃（新干线・特急除外）
祭典・观光街即使是「食べ歩き」摊位，也建议在摊位附近吃完
瓶装饮料边走边喝相对宽松，但人多的商店街也不推荐
pointsEn: Most konbini have a standing counter or bench outside — finish there
Avoid eating in stations, on platforms, or in trains (shinkansen/express excepted)
Even at festival or tourist street stalls, eat near the stand before moving on
Bottled drinks while walking are more tolerated, but still avoid on crowded streets
tipZh: 「食べ歩き」一词在观光语境下也可指「巡店式品尝」（不是所有场景都=失礼），但单纯「边走边咬」的失礼感全国共通。
tipEn: "Tabearuki" can also mean "food crawl" in tourist areas — context matters, but eating while literally walking is universally seen as rude.
